_sources.discrepancies: NCSL lists SC as 'photo ID requested' (non-strict). SoS confirms voters without photo ID may vote a provisional ballot and return with photo ID within 48 hours, or sign an affidavit with a reasonable impediment. Consistent.

earlyVoting.notes: Early voting (in-person absentee) is available at county election offices. Hours and locations vary. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting for runoff. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
Early voting available at county election offices. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
In-person absentee (early voting) is available at county election offices beginning about two weeks before Election Day.

acceptedIds: South Carolina Voter Registration Card with photo
Employee badge or ID card issued by a state or federal government agency
Student photo ID from a South Carolina public college or university
votingRules.expirationRule: Photo ID must not be expired. TODO verify for 2026 cycle.
votingRules.impedimentDeclaration: Voters without acceptable photo ID may cast a provisional ballot and either present ID within 2 business days, or sign an affidavit stating a reasonable impediment to obtaining photo ID.
votingRules.phonesAtPollsDetail: South Carolina prohibits use of wireless communication devices in the voting booth. You may not photograph your ballot.
additionalRules: Open primary: any registered voter may choose which party's primary to participate in.
Runoff rule: if no candidate receives a majority in the primary, the top two advance to a runoff. A voter who voted in one party's primary may only vote in that party's runoff.

eligibility: Will be absent from the county on Election Day
Physical illness or disability preventing appearance
Attending to a person who is ill or disabled
Observing a religious holiday on Election Day
Workers required at workplace all polling hours
voteByMail.applicationDeadline: Must be received by the county board 4 days before the election
voteByMail.returnDeadline: Must be received by the county board by 7 p.m. on Election Day
voteByMail.returnDeadlinePlain: Absentee ballot must arrive at the county board by 7 p.m. on Election Day.

runoffRules.ruleExplanation: South Carolina rule: if you voted in one party's primary, you can only vote in that same party's runoff. If you did not vote in the primary, you may choose either party's runoff.
